content.full_text:
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

December 4, 1986

Mr. Dennis John Gianatassio
Direct Services
121 Golden Gate Avenue
San Francisco, CA 94102

RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-86-290

Dear Mr. Gianatassio:

In your letter postmarked November 3, 1986, you requested
advice concerning the campaign disclosure provisions of the
Political Reform Act.1/

FACTS

You are a candidate for elective office in San Francisco.
Friends of yours are providing you with shelter, food and
transportation. Their intention is to support you personally,
not because of your status as a candidate.

QUESTION

By providing you with shelter, food and transportation, are
your friends making in-kind (non-monetary) contributions on
behalf of your candidacy?

CONCLUSION

The shelter, food and transportation provided to you are not
considered expenditures for political purposes and need not be
reported on your campaign disclosure statements.

ANALYSIS

The term "contribution" is defined in Government Code
Section 82015: "'Contribution' means a payment, a forgiveness
of a loan, a payment of a loan by a third party, or an
enforceable promise to make a payment except to the extent that
full and adequate consideration is received unless it is clear
from the surrounding circumstances that it is not made for
political purposes...."

_____________________________

1/Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.

Mr. Gianatassio
Page Two


     Since the shelter, food and transportation provided to you
was a personal gift rather than a campaign contribution, the
value of the shelter, food and transportation need not be
reported on your candidate and officeholder campaign statement
(Form 430).

     You stated in your letter that you enclosed a copy of your
final pre-election statement, Form 430.  However, the enclosure
was not received with your letter.

     Please contact me if I may be of further assistance.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        Diane M. Griffiths
                                        General Counsel

                                        [signature]
                         By             Mary Ann Kvasager
                                        Political Reform Consultant

DMG:MAK:cah

DENNIS JOHN GIANATASSIO
% DIRECT SERVICES
121 GLIDDEN GATE AVE.
SAN FRANCISCO, CA 94102

FPPC

NOV 4 3:43PM '86

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
% LEGAL DIVISION
ATT: DIANNE GRIFFITHS
428 J STREET
SACRAMENTO, CA. 95814

DEAR MS GRIFFITHS,

I am enclosing this letter to you with my final pre-election statement as a summary statement regarding questions of non-monetary support for my campaign (as refered to in my previous letter of October 6, 1986) as well as other questions arising from the unique aspect of my candidacy. I am filing this letter together with my form 420 so that these questions may be eased to public scrutiny, etc...

First, it should be clearly acknowledged that many friends have assisted me in my campaign in a very personal, informal manner including, at times, providing Shelter, food, transportation, etc... This has been, as stated, support of me personally - and yet, I cannot separate it from my political campaign since the two he welded.

[handwritten marks and annotations in margin]

This support has been, in a very real way, invaluable, since it springs from a personal well-spring which extends to support of my candidacy as well. To divide the personal from the political is impractical. Still, I want to emphasize that I am enclosing this letter so that my record may be "open to the public." And, of course, I am willing to follow-up regarding these questions with you. (Please note that these questions also, to a much lesser extent, apply to expenditures as well).

Because the campaign is in its final week I would like to defer any unnecessary further elucidation of these matters until the election is over. I assume that I am most willing to work with you regarding these matters. Finally I would like to state that I have made an effort to be conscientious about these matters; I do not feel I have evaded fully all reasonable diligence, as stated in the verification, but I am prepared, if necessary, to provide more detailed information with your assistance.

Thank you for your time and consideration,

[illegible signature]

% DIRECT SERVICES
121 GOLDEN GATE AVE.
SAN FRANCISCO, CA. 94102
OCTOBER 6, 1986

FPPC
Fair Political Practices Commission OCT 9 3 40 PM '86
To Legal Division

ATTN: Dianne Griffiths

829 J STREET

SACRAMENTO, CA. 95814

Dear Ms. Griffiths,

I am writing to you as requested by staff members of the Fair Political Practices Commission in regards to questions about what constitutes a "political situation." Since it is my understanding that staff members have discussed my questionnaire and conversed with you, I will not go into detail here. Please accept this letter as a "formal inquiry" (as requested of staff) especially with regard to a confrontation of ones received from my parents, and also regarding some questions regarding "non-monetary support" received - I will contact the Commission by phone as follow-up.

Respectfully,

L. [illegible] Montanez
Personnel

embedding.qa_text: This is a letter from the California Fair Political Practices Commission dated December 4, 1986, addressed to Mr. Dennis John Gianatassio at Direct Services, 121 Golden Gate Avenue, San Francisco, CA 94102. The letter responds to Mr. Gianatassio's request for advice regarding the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act. The Commission provides the following analysis: **FACTS** - You are a candidate for elective office in San Francisco. - Friends of yours are providing you with shelter, food and transportation. - Their intention is to support you personally, not because of your status as a candidate. **QUESTION** - By providing you with shelter, food and transportation, are your friends making in-kind (non-monetary) contributions on behalf of your candidacy? **CONCLUSION** - The shelter, food and transportation provided to you are not considered expenditures for political purposes and need not be reported on your campaign disclosure statements. **ANALYSIS** - The term "contribution" is defined in Government Code Section 82015: "'Contribution' means a payment, a forgiveness of a loan, a payment of a loan by a third party, or an enforceable promise to make a payment except to the extent that full and adequate consideration is received unless it is clear from the surrounding circumstances that it is not made for political purposes...." **REFERENCE** 1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Mr. Gianatassio Since the shelter, food and transportation provided to you was a personal gift rather than a campaign contribution, the value of the shelter, food and transportation need not be reported on your candidate and officeholder campaign statement (Form 430). You stated in your letter that you enclosed a copy of your final pre-election statement, Form 430. However, the enclosure was not received with your letter. Please contact me if I may be of further assistance. Sincerely, Diane M. Griffiths General Counsel By Mary Ann Kvasager Political Reform Consultant The handwritten letter is addressed to the Fair Political Practices Commission, Legal Division, and signed by Dennis John Gianatassio. The letter discusses his final pre-election statement regarding questions of non-monetary support for his campaign, as referenced in his previous letter of October 6, 1986. It also mentions other questions arising from the unique aspect of his candidacy and states that he is filing this letter together with his Form 430 so that these questions may be open to public scrutiny.
